essence: Maintains and certifies the specialist equipment used by fire and rescue crews to ensure operational safety and readiness.

identity.title: Fire Service Equipment Technician
identity.summary: Inspects, services, and repairs fire‑and‑rescue equipment including breathing apparatus, hoses, ladders, pumps, rescue tools, and PPE.

salary.notes: Technicians specialising in breathing apparatus, hydraulic rescue tools, or pump systems can exceed £45k-£55k+.

qualifications: Technical training in equipment maintenance or mechanical engineering
Knowledge of fire‑and‑rescue tools, PPE, and breathing apparatus standards
Ability to test, calibrate, and certify safety‑critical equipment
Understanding of fire‑service operational procedures and safety protocols

lifestyle.description: Fire service equipment technicians work in workshops, stations, and training centres maintaining essential firefighting gear. They test breathing apparatus, service hoses and pumps, repair ladders, maintain rescue tools, and ensure PPE meets strict safety standards. The role suits individuals who enjoy technical precision, safety‑critical work, and supporting frontline firefighters.

habits: Follows strict safety and maintenance protocols
Keeps detailed service and compliance records
Communicates clearly with fire crews and officers
Maintains an organised and well‑equipped workshop
